title: 113 Mathematics Observati O n
subject: Mathematics
objective:
To make   a parallelogram, rectangle, square and trapezium using
set squares.
materials:
Four pieces of 30 °   - 90 °   - 60 °   set squares and four
pieces of 45 °   - 90 °   - 45 °   set squares, cardboard,
white paper, pen/pencil, paper, eraser.
procedure:
1.   Take a piece of cardboard of a convenient size and paste a white paper
on it.
2.   Arrange different sets of set squares as shown in Figures 1 to 5 and
trace the boundary of the figure using pen/pencil.
Fig. 1
Fig. 2   Fig. 3
discussion:
1.   This activity may be used in explaining the meaning of bisector of an
angle.
2.   This activity can also be used in finding bisectors of angles of a triangle
and to show that they meet at a point.
114 Laboratory Manual – Elementary Stage
